title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what you think the purpose of prison should be and whether rehabilitation programmes might be effective.

images.label: A
images.description: A prison workshop where inmates are learning carpentry skills under supervision, working with wood and tools in a purposeful atmosphere

images.label: B
images.description: A long, bleak prison corridor with rows of locked cell doors, a lone guard walking past, the harsh lighting emphasising the institutional environment

followUp.question: Do you think the priority of the justice system should be punishment or rehabilitation? Why?

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what approaches to crime prevention might be most effective and what role communities play.

images.label: D
images.description: Police officers walking a neighbourhood beat, stopping to chat with local residents outside a corner shop in a friendly, community-focused manner

images.label: E
images.description: A youth club in a deprived area, teenagers engaged in art, sport and workshops run by community youth workers, looking engaged and motivated

followUp.question: Do you think investing in community programmes is more effective at reducing crime than harsher sentencing? What is your reasoning?
